[[File:Ivandis flail detail.png|left|170px]]
The '''Ivandis flail''' is a one-handed [[melee]] weapon created by the [[Player character|player]] during [[A Taste of Hope]] from a [[chain]], an [[enchanted emerald sickle (b)]] and the [[rod of Ivandis]]. The flail requires an [[Attack]] level of 40 to wield, and gives a 20% damage bonus against [[vampyre]]s. It can also [[autocast]] [[standard spellbook]] spells.

This flail is effective in damaging and killing the [[vyrewatch]] because, despite their sufficient mind-reading ability to predict attacks and avoid damage, the flail is incredibly unpredictable and random. The sickle head further increases the unpredictability of the weapon. Thus, attackers do not hit the Vyrewatch in the intended way, rendering their mind-reading useless.

The flail retains the ability to cast [[Bloom]] from the silver sickle (b). This versatility lets a player kill [[ghast]]s, [[vampyre juvinate]]s and [[vyrewatch]] with a single weapon.

While players can make a flail themselves after the quest, it is much easier to purchase one from [[Vertida Sefalatis]] for 20,000 coins in the old [[Myreque Hideout]] under [[Old Man Ral]]'s home or from [[Perdu]] for 30,000 coins from the [[Lost Property shop]]. During [[Sins of the Father]], an Ivandis flail is used to create a [[Blisterwood flail]] in the hideout.

==Special attack==
[[File:Retainer (flail).gif|thumb|220px|A player performs the Ivandis flail's special attack, ''Retainer''.]]
The Ivandis flail has a special attack, ''Retainer'', which traps a [[vampyre juvenile]] or [[vampyre juvinate|juvinate]] that has less than 50% hitpoints remaining, preventing it from retaliating for 30 seconds. This consumes 10% of the player's special attack energy.

Even if the attack fails to activate, stating either {{mes|This spell can only be used on vampyre juveniles or vampyre juvinates.}} or {{mes|This spell only works against vampyres with less than half of their health remaining.}}, it will still incur the normal attack delay, so take care not to accidentally use it in those circumstances.

==Treasure Trails==
{{FaloItem|Ivandis flail|Vampyres are agile opponents, damaged best with a weapon of many components.}} Alternatively, a [[Rod of ivandis]] or [[Blisterwood flail]] can be used.
